2021-05-01.title: Global Virus Cases Reach New Peak, Driven by India and South America

2021-05-02.abstract: The social networking app is booming in authoritarian countries, where users are speaking freely about otherwise taboo topics.

2021-05-04.abstract: The accident occurred in the city’s southeast, killing more than 20 and injuring at least 70 others, the authorities said.

2021-05-05.abstract: In much of the developed world, vaccine orders are soaring into the billions of doses, coronavirus cases are easing and economies are poised to roar back to life. In many less developed nations, though, the virus is raging on, while vaccinations are far too slow to protect even the most vulnerable.

2021-05-06.abstract: The European Union said it was considering the proposal but did not endorse it. Germany said that the move could undermine the production of vaccines, deterring E.U. consideration of the plan.

2021-05-24.abstract: Ms. Aung San Suu Kyi, 75, faces a raft of charges after being detained by Myanmar’s military. If found guilty, she could be imprisoned for life.

2021-05-25.abstract: The E.U. moves to restrict Belarus’s air access.

2021-05-27.abstract: Dozens of besieged outposts or bases, and four district centers, have given up to the insurgents this month, in an accelerating rural collapse as American troops leave.

2021-05-27.title: Hong Kong Has a New Type of Prisoner: Pro-Democracy Activists
2021-05-27.abstract: Hundreds of protesters, many students or college-educated, face potentially stiff sentences after Beijing’s crackdown. Those behind bars already battle isolation and disillusionment.

2021-05-27.title: A lockdown in Victoria State, Australia, follows a variant-fueled outbreak.
2021-05-27.abstract: After nearly three months without an infection, health officials have found 26 cases in the Melbourne area. Critics blamed ill-equipped quarantine facilities and a slow vaccine rollout.

2021-05-28.title: U.N. Rights Council to Investigate Israeli-Palestinian Violence
2021-05-28.abstract: The United Nations’ top human rights body voted on Thursday to open an inquiry into abuses and possible war crimes committed in Israel and the occupied Palestinian territories during recent hostilities.

2021-05-28.title: Taiwan Prays for Rain and Scrambles to Save Water
2021-05-28.abstract: Some of the island’s lakes and reservoirs have nearly run dry. And water restrictions have forced many residents to modify how they shower, wash dishes and flush.

2021-05-28.title: From Protester to Prisoner: How Hong Kong Is Stifling Dissent
2021-05-28.abstract: Many of the city’s democracy activists face jail terms amid a broad campaign to subdue opposition.

2021-05-28.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-05-28.abstract: An investigation into U.S. election disinformation.

2021-05-28.title: Canadian Lawmaker Is Exposed on Camera for a Second Time
2021-05-28.abstract: After appearing naked during a virtual House of Commons session in April, a member of Parliament said that he urinated while on video this week.

2021-05-29.title: Zimbabwe Authorities Arrest Local Reporter Working for The New York Times
2021-05-29.abstract: Jeffrey Moyo, a freelance journalist for The Times who is based in Harare, was being held on charges of violating the country’s immigration laws. His lawyers say the accusation is spurious.

2021-05-29.title: A Week of Big Blows for Big Oil
2021-05-29.abstract: Two victories by environmental activists added to the uncertainty surrounding the industry and Canada’s oil sands.

2021-05-29.title: U.S. to Reimpose Sanctions on Belarus Over Forced Plane Landing
2021-05-29.abstract: The move is the latest Western response to the forced landing of a European commercial flight with a Belarusian dissident aboard.

2021-05-29.title: They Call It ‘Insane’: Where Myanmar Sends Political Prisoners
2021-05-29.abstract: For 134 years, Insein Prison has stood as a monument to brutality. Since the Feb. 1 coup, journalists, elected leaders and pro-democracy protesters have been held in the aging facility.

2021-05-29.title: Plague of Mice in Australia Overruns Farms, Shops and Bedrooms
2021-05-29.abstract: For half a year, the rodents have been chewing their way around the country’s eastern grain belt, leaving economic and psychological scars.

2021-05-30.title: A Nashville hat store sold ‘Not Vaccinated’ Star of David patches.
2021-05-30.abstract: Protesters gathered on Saturday outside a hat store in Nashville that sold “not vaccinated” Star of David patches and compared vaccine passports to the Nazi practice of requesting “your papers.”

2021-05-30.title: As Covid Rampages Across Nepal, Workers Pay the Price
2021-05-30.abstract: The Himalayan country is considering declaring a health emergency to help contain a second wave that experts say migrant laborers brought back from India.

2021-05-30.title: ‘Good for the Soul’: Giant Murals Turn São Paulo Into Open Air Gallery
2021-05-30.abstract: Officials in São Paulo, Brazil, once hounded graffiti artists and muralists, treating them as vandals. Now the city champions, and even funds, their art, and it’s everywhere and supersized.

2021-05-30.title: Iran Will Try French Citizen on Spying Charges
2021-05-30.abstract: Benjamin Brière stands accused of flying a drone and taking photographs in a prohibited area. His lawyer said Iran would put him on trial for espionage, which can carry the death penalty.

2021-05-30.title: A 6-Year-Old Was Chained and Hungry in a Syrian Camp. Then She Died.
2021-05-30.abstract: Nahla al-Othman lived in a tent camp for displaced Syrians where her father often chained her to stop her from wandering. She was malnourished and choked to death while eating too quickly.

2021-05-31.title: ​North Korea Accuses U.S. of ‘Shameful Double-Dealing’ on Missile Policy
2021-05-31.abstract: In its first reaction to President Biden’s summit with President Moon Jae-in of South Korea, North Korean state media warned of an arms race.

2021-05-31.title: China Says It Will Allow Couples to Have 3 Children, Up From 2
2021-05-31.abstract: The move is the Communist Party’s latest attempt to reverse declining birthrates and avert a population crisis, but experts say it is woefully inadequate.

2021-05-31.title: On the Covid Front Lines, When Not Getting Belly Rubs
2021-05-31.abstract: In Thailand and around the world, dogs are being trained to sniff out the coronavirus in people. So far, the results have been impressive.

2021-05-31.title: A Siege, a Supply Run and a Descent Into a Decade-Old Battle
2021-05-31.abstract: ‘Do you have body armor?’ the Afghan pilot asked us as we prepared for a trip to outposts surrounded by the Taliban.
